comment.body: That’s why I thank Alma for the work she does. I’m not paying to have the dish served ready at the table (go long or go short); I’m paying for valuable information, to fill in my blind spot, and to engage with people smarter than me who offer different perspectives and insights. That’s what makes it worth the price. Where I come from, we say: spend time with people better than you and pay for their meal. It’s about respect — it’s not about being right or wrong, especially since no one has a crystal ball.

comment.body:
After reading Alma's comments above I wanted to make a general request: can we all just commit to encourage her and not cast doubt about her posts, calls, etc?  

I think we can all agree that Alma's insights and quant capabilities are truly extraordinary. Beyond that, the level of effort she give is SECOND TO NONE. Yes, we're "clients" of hers but doing this work is a side gig for her and she gives us content that is beyond valuable vs what we pay.  She can be doing lots of better things with her time and I think the vast majority of us would love it if she continued longer vs shorter.  And I think encouraging her would help seal that.

comment.body:
Right I agree. But you didn’t mention that “risk” until today’s post. I just think we might be getting too caught up into subjective, narrative driven feedback loops that tend to project certainty and extreme conviction when the outcome isn’t necessarily that certain. Hence why I stated a lot of this geo political context is very speculative and subjective.

This is also why I’ve been mentioned my counter argument, such as western “political division” and volatility making it tough for rooted conservative cultures to unite with US based on deeper values and ideology (especially without figuring out the EU situation). That’s certainly what has been hinted by calling it the “Holy Alliance 2.0” and you mentioned these cultural connections when you first introduced the concept.

Just seems like the most sensible approach would be for these states to stay independent and leverage both side’s weaknesses to their own benefit, and keep their options open.

I also think, which I know you spend a lot of time investing into these geo political narratives, that it shouldn’t take up such a large portion of your bandwidth, especially since it is so speculative. I’d rather have you invest your energy into teaching about Stochastic Volatility through more interactive educational means like videos/screen sharing/etc.

But it is your channel and I’m not trying to dictate how you run it, just providing some feedback.

Also, I have acknowledged your talent, and you get alot of praise here, so I’m not sure why I would have to acknowledge it constantly if what you said earlier is directed at me.

At least 10 members here call you “mommy” on a daily basis 😂

Your calls you have mentioned recently have been great. Is that a result of your ability to read the tape or put together these geopolitical narratives? I’d say it’s more of the former.

comment.body:
50/50 is for the market.

the holy alliance is an offer.
they have common ideological roots now, but a past full of conflicts. this is why the negotiations in jeedah are moving slowly.
but as much as i hear, they are moving forward.
but until then the countries keep their businesses running, and it is brics.

now, the investors want Trump to fight stagflation, then they will save the economy by buying debt, and tech etc.
but now the uncertainity is very high about the outcomes.
these games... so... nothing is above these plp in terms of power, so everything depends on their decisions and miscalculations.

I don't understand either fully why they are surprised: they asked for it, they knew that recession is coming, they knew that this will be like this, and we have one week until Easter.
but they lose a lot of money. Investors are net long on the economy. Not short. And they lose a lot of money.

And there can be a lot of factors that play out differently in the near-future. Factors that nobody could have calculated forward properly (in terms of probability).
and if the party is better in the other house, they will join that one. 

they don't allies yet!
